title: podman push error to quay.io w/o quotes
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

kind bug

**Description**

I suspect this to be a CLI parsing issue.  A push to quay.io of an image without quotes around it fails.  

```
Using https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/contrib/podmanimage/stable/Dockerfile as ~Dockerfile.podmanstable

# podman build -f ~/Dockerfile.podmanstable -t stable:v1.4.4 .

# podman push stable:v1.4.4 quay.io/podman/stable:v1.4.4
Getting image source signatures
ERRO[0000] error unmounting /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/6a89ca8a49ec35ae8f5dd9f276255a166ab8cf5b4320186d3d8d73a825f00803/merged: invalid argument 
Copying blob acecadfb52b1 done
Copying blob 9f712b91f27e done
Copying blob 9bdbc6d012f2 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 5ce23ae9a223 skipped: already exists
Error: Error copying image to the remote destination: Error reading blob sha256:b344bbcb001884791e33e5e51e5c71d42d3f44c3d7b78dec25ac06a7b04d2cba: error creating overlay mount to /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/6a89ca8a49ec35ae8f5dd9f276255a166ab8cf5b4320186d3d8d73a825f00803/merged: device or resource busy

# podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE  COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS  NAMES


# buildah images
REPOSITORY                 TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED        SIZE
localhost/stable           v1.4.4   0ae4f1f0bb0f   13 hours ago   414 MB
docker.io/library/fedora   latest   2b74bf3d2430   35 hours ago   255 MB

# podman push stable:v1.4.4 'quay.io/podman/stable:v1.4.4'
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob b344bbcb0018 done
Copying blob 5ce23ae9a223 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 9bdbc6d012f2 skipped: already exists
Copying blob acecadfb52b1 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 9f712b91f27e skipped: already exists
Copying config 0ae4f1f0bb done
Writing manifest to image destination
Copying config 0ae4f1f0bb done
Writing manifest to image destination
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

See description

**Describe the results you received:**
See description

**Describe the results you expected:**

The push to work with or without the quotes around the image name.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
# podman version
Version:            1.4.2-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.2
Git Commit:         e22f91bd28082d2fa7b27d9d17684482da41c889
Built:              Tue Jun 18 16:42:05 2019
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
# podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: e22f91bd28082d2fa7b27d9d17684482da41c889
  go version: go1.12.2
  podman version: 1.4.2-dev
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.4.2-1.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 0.2.0, commit: d7234dc01ae2ef08c42e3591e876723ad1c914c9'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "30"
  MemFree: 2248400896
  MemTotal: 4134051840
  OCIRuntime:
    package: containerd.io-1.2.6-3.3.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc8
      commit: 425e105d5a03fabd737a126ad93d62a9eeede87f
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 2683027456
  SwapTotal: 2684350464
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 1
  hostname: localhost.localdomain
  kernel: 5.1.15-300.fc30.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 19h 53m 28.52s (Approximately 0.79 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mountopt=nodev,metacopy=on
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 6
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Fedora 30 VM

comments.body: I can't reproduce this on master. @TomSweeneyRedHat, could you paste the command and output?
UGH, it's in the commented out description...  Will update.

@TomSweeneyRedHat 

>   OCIRuntime:
>     package: containerd.io-1.2.6-3.3.fc30.x86_64

I do find this a little weird. Did you have containerd installed and not runc?
This seems to work fine for me with `podman-1.4.4-4.fc30.x86_64`

Still the same error for me on a newly install F29 VM  with

```
# rpm -qa |grep podman
podman-manpages-1.4.4-4.fc29.noa
```

The only thing I put on the VM was this rpm and whatever it dragged down with it.
Hm. F29? That could be it. Maybe dependencies?

OK, getting weirder.  I tried on F30 today and still the same issue, but I noted that given these images:

```
# podman images
REPOSITORY                 TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED         SIZE
localhost/stable           v1.4.4   bbe3f01fd55b   4 minutes ago   413 MB
docker.io/library/fedora   latest   ef49352c9c21   4 days ago      253 MB
```
If I did:

`podman push stable:v1.4.4 ..` first it failed.  Regardless of the back ticks around the target.  However if I did either:

```
podman push bbe3f01fd55b ..   #imageID   

Or
podman push localhost/stable:v1.4.4 ..  #full name
```

Then everything worked fine.  After the push succeeded, if I tried the push again that failed with just `stable:v1.4.4` then that would succeed as well.  I'm not sure why the failing push works only after the fully specified one does.?

I don't think the quotes can make any difference, they are evaluated by the shell before they are passed to Podman as arguments.

I am hitting the same error right now both with podman 1.4.4 and from master

Yeah still happening for me.  I'm not sure about the quotes helping/hurting.  I do know that I ran into this again this past weekend.  I do know that if I persist and repush, it eventually goes through.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
Are you still seeing the issue, @TomSweeneyRedHat ?
Weird that we have never heard about this from anyone else.
It was replicating quite a bit 1.4 timeframe, but I've not run into it in the recent past that I can recall.  will close.  Sweeney luck I guess.
